page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core configuration, providing 16 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities but weak single-core execution. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the bottom 25% tier, and single-threaded gzip compression is in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests, consistently register in the middle 50% average tier. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached small-block reads at 657.2 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 76.43 GB/sec, both aligning with average performance tiers.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1.4xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high single-threaded CPU performance. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable execution for multi-threaded tasks, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage integration. This profile is positioned for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded software compilation, while being less optimal for single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.4xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores (16 vCPUs) powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics consistently perform in the average middle 50% tier. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It is positioned for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation, but is less effective for single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for database operations and caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical cores on Intel Xeon 8269CY architecture
128.0 GB of RAM providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core performance contrasted with weak single-core execution
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
